title: Erigeron tetrapleurus
intro: Erigeron tetrapleurus, also known as four-leafed fleabane, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the sunflower family (Asteraceae) that is native to North America. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory problems, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Erigeron tetrapleurus include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat colds, coughs, and sore throats, as well as to reduce fever and alleviate pain.
